sales.title: CARIA. Aphrodisias. Salonina (AD 254-268). AE (22mm, 6.63 gm, 7h). NGC Choice VF 5/5 - 2/5.  ΙΟΥ ΚΟΡ-ϹΑΛΩΝΙΝΑ, draped bust of Salonina right, set on crescent, hair weaved in rows with long plait carried up the back of head and tucked under stephane / ΑΦΡΟ-Δ-ΕΙ-ϹΙ/ΕΩΝ, three nude athletes drawing lots from amphora set on ground. RPC X Online Unassigned 60145.  Ex Ancient Byways, private sale with old dealer's tag included.   Wife of Emperor Gallienus, little is known about Salonina. Her connection to the political world is debated, scholars dividedly tying her to Publius Cornelius Saecularis, Cornelia Gallonia, and Julia Paula. What is known is that she married Gallienus a decade before his reign and together they had three sons: Valerian II, Saloninus, and Marinianus. However, after her husbands death in AD 268, her fate remains unknown.    HID05401242017  © 2026 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
sales.description: Heritage Auctions, Inc. | ['Auction #61605']

sales.title:
Gallienus, Sole Reign (AD 253-268). BI antoninianus (21mm, 3.80 gm, 11h). NGC MS 4/5 - 3/5, Silvering. Antioch. GALLIENVS AVG, radiate, draped, and cuirassed bust of Gallienus right, seen from behind / AEQVTAS AVG, Aequitas standing facing, head left, scales in outstretched right hand, cornucopia cradled in left; star in left field. RIC V.I (Asia) 627. Water-like surfaces with crisp detail. From the Werner Collection. Ex Heritage Auctions, Auction 232211 (16 March 2022), lot 63265. Publius Licinius Egnatius Gallienus proudly holds the title of longest ruling emperor during the Crisis of the Third Century. In a time of chaos and the potential collapse of the Empire as a whole, Gallienus stands out as an emperor who made significant reforms, including strengthening the cavalry and relying more on professional soldiers over senatorial generals. He also supported the arts and promoted a more centralized imperial authority. Though he managed to fend off several external threats, his rule was plagued by internal rebellion, and he was ultimately assassinated by his own officers.Gallienus's coinage is notable for its rapid debasement throughout his reign, Early reign issues being much finer and fully silvered, to his later coinage being little more than lightly silvered base metal pieces. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice
